violations.text: Molluscan Shellfish (oysters) located in the members building reach-in did not have the proper identification tags. Molluscan Shellstock tags must remain attached to the container in which the Molluscan Shellstock are received until the container is empty. Shellstock tags must then be maintained for 90 days in chronological order with the date the last shellfish was used/sold noted on the tag. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

Foods and deli meats/cheeses observed not marked with the date of preparation/slicing. Ready-to-eat time/temperature control for safety (TCS) foods prepared/sliced in the establishment and held for more than 24 hours must be marked with the date of preparation/slicing. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025
Spray bottle used for storing chemicals taken from bulk supplies was not marked with the common name of the chemical. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance of the main kitchen and the entrance of the members building, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. New Violation. Correct By: 05/31/2025

The ice scoop is stored on an unclean surface (top of the ice machine) between uses. During pauses in food preparation, utensils must be stored on a clean surface or in a clean, protected location. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 05/20/2025

Food contact surfaces of equipment/utensils (can opener) were observed with an accumulation of food and debris. The food contact surfaces of equipment must be clean to sight and touch. Corrected On-Site. New Violation. Correct By: 06/08/2023

The food-contact surfaces of equipment and utensils were not sanitized. The required utensil surface temperature of 160 degrees Fahrenheit was not achieved during the sanitizing cycle of the dishwasher as evidenced by the failure of the thermolabel (temperature indicator) affixed to the surface of a utensil to turn black. Service call was placed. Utensils/kitchenware can be washed and rinsed in the main dishwasher but must be sanitized using the 3-bay sink or small dishwasher until the main dishwasher is reaching proper sanitizing temperature. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023

The doors to the outside, located at the entrance of the member's building, are not tight-fitting. Outer openings of a food establishment must be protected against the entry of insects and rodents by tight-fitting doors. All exterior doors are to be checked to see if they are tight-fitting and be repaired if necessary. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023

The facility is lacking thermo labels or similar internal temperature indicating device for the dish machine in the main kitchen and the member's building kitchen. New Violation. Correct By: 06/16/2023
